leagueBriefing: Conference play kicked off with a bang on Thursday as top-ranked teams faced early tests and offensive records were nearly shattered. Georgia Tech sent a clear message to the ACC with a dominant shutout of Clemson, while Wichita State put on a historic clinic at the plate. In the Big 12, BYU proved that home-field advantage is real by halting one of the nation's longest active winning streaks.

games.headline: Georgia Tech silences Clemson in dominant conference opener
games.brief: Georgia Tech made a massive statement to open ACC play, absolutely dismantling No. 11 Clemson in a game that was shortened to seven innings due to the run rule. The Yellow Jackets' pitching staff kept the Tigers completely off balance, surrendering only two hits while the offense relentlessly piled on runs. It was the kind of performance that shifts expectations in a division race, proving Tech can travel into a hostile environment and dominate a top-tier rival.
bullets: Yellow Jacket pitchers combined for a two-hit shutout, stifling a Clemson offense that entered the game with high momentum.
The game was called after seven innings as Georgia Tech reached a 10-run lead, invoking the conference run rule.
Georgia Tech's offense was clinical, racking up 12 hits while capitalizing on two Clemson errors.
This victory moves Georgia Tech to a 16-2 record, further solidifying their status as one of the most dangerous teams in the country.
games.context: The dominant shutout serves as a major momentum shifter for Georgia Tech as they begin their ACC campaign against a top-15 opponent.

games.headline: Wichita State flirts with history in 27-run explosion against Butler
games.brief: Wichita State turned their series opener into a batting practice session, scoring their most runs in nearly a quarter-century to blow past Butler. After Butler took a brief early lead, the Shockers responded with a relentless barrage, highlighted by an 11-run third inning where they batted around without recording an out. It was a historic night at Eck Stadium that saw WSU score in every single inning they came to the plate, leaving the Bulldogs searching for answers.
bullets: The 27 runs scored are the most by the Shockers since 2002 and the second-most in program history.
Max Kaufer continued his torrid start to the season, blasting two home runs to bring his season total to 11 through 18 games.
Wichita State scored in every inning (1st through 6th), a feat the program hadn't achieved since 1999.
Owen Washburn extended his impressive multi-hit game streak to 10 straight contests with a 2-for-4 performance.
The Shocker offense benefited from 12 walks and three hit batsmen issued by a parade of six Butler pitchers.
games.context: The offensive explosion nearly broke the school record of 35 runs and marks a definitive turnaround for a Shocker program looking to return to national relevance.

games.headline: BYU power surge ends Cincinnati’s seven-game win streak
games.brief: In a classic Big 12 battle, BYU used the long ball and stellar pitching to snap Cincinnati's red-hot seven-game winning streak. The game was a pitcher's duel through the early stages until the Cougars' bats woke up with two pivotal home runs that provided all the cushion they needed. While the Bearcats attempted a ninth-inning rally to keep their streak alive, BYU's bullpen slammed the door to secure a vital win in their conference home opener.
bullets: Ezra McNaughton delivered the decisive blow with a three-run home run in the bottom of the seventh inning to break the game open.
Crew McChesney opened the scoring for the Cougars with a two-run blast in the fourth inning.
BYU starter Garrison Sumner earned the win, allowing only one run and four hits while striking out six over 5.1 innings.
The loss was the first for Cincinnati in over two weeks, ending a seven-game stretch of dominant baseball.
The victory marked head coach Trent Pratt’s 200th career game at the helm for BYU.
games.context: This victory gives BYU an early edge in the Big 12 standings while halting one of the most significant winning streaks in Cincinnati program history.
